pattern: body text does not match declared name/description
description: Agent declares name=config-safety-reviewer and description referencing configuration safety, but body opens 'You are an expert code reviewer' and describes general code review. This is an unupdated copy of the old @code-reviewer template from v2.4
suggested_fix: Rewrite agent body to focus on configuration safety: database connection strings, timeout values, environment variable validation, deployment config review, and infrastructure capacity matching

description: Command documented in CLAUDE.md as a v2.7.0 production feature but the .md file does not exist; users invoking /docs-gen will get 'command not found'
suggested_fix: Create the command file or remove references to /docs-gen from CLAUDE.md

description: Command documented in CLAUDE.md as a v2.7.0 production feature but the .md file does not exist; users invoking /test-gen will get 'command not found'
suggested_fix: Create the command file or remove references to /test-gen from CLAUDE.md

description: todo-check.md is an exact duplicate of check-todos.md; maintaining two copies risks divergence and confuses users about which name is canonical
suggested_fix: Remove todo-check.md; keep check-todos.md as the canonical command per v2.7 naming

description: todo-add.md is an exact duplicate of add-to-todos.md
suggested_fix: Remove todo-add.md; keep add-to-todos.md as the canonical command per v2.7 naming

description: prompt-run.md is an exact duplicate of run-prompt.md
suggested_fix: Remove prompt-run.md; keep run-prompt.md as the canonical command

description: whats-next.md is functionally identical to handoff-create.md with only the framework name changed in one heading; both implement the same handoff document creation workflow
suggested_fix: Remove whats-next.md or make it a distinct command if TÂCHES workflow differs meaningfully from Tresor workflow

description: prompt-create.md is functionally identical to create-prompt.md with minor header differences
suggested_fix: Remove prompt-create.md; keep create-prompt.md as the canonical command

pattern: TodoWrite used in body but not declared in allowed-tools

suggested_fix: Add TodoWrite to the allowed-tools list in the frontmatter

pattern: severely underdeveloped command body (65 lines vs 700-1100 for peer commands)
description: debt-analysis.md body consists of one Phase 1 section with a brief example output and no agent prompt templates, no phase chaining logic, no numbered steps, and no output format section; all other orchestration commands are 700-1100+ lines
suggested_fix: Expand debt-analysis.md to match the quality of other orchestration commands: add Phase 0 planning, detailed agent prompt templates for each of the 3 agents, output format specification, and numbered phase steps

pattern: stray double-quote in JavaScript pseudo-code: activeForm": instead of activeForm:
description: Line 712-713 contain a JS syntax error (extra double-quote in object key) in the TodoWrite call. This is pseudo-code so it won't execute, but it signals copy-paste issues
suggested_fix: Change 'activeForm": "RCA completed"' to 'activeForm: "RCA completed"' on lines 712-713

description: Body contains 5+ vague quantifiers that reduce specificity: 'comprehensive review', 'various aspects', 'multiple issues', 'appropriate fixes'
suggested_fix: Replace vague quantifiers with specific, measurable language

pattern: comprehensive, various, multiple, robust, detailed (5+ instances)
description: Command body uses 5+ vague quantifiers across phase descriptions and output format
suggested_fix: Replace vague quantifiers with specific language

description: Command body uses 5+ vague quantifiers
suggested_fix: Replace vague quantifiers with specific language

pattern: missing name: field in command frontmatter
description: review.md frontmatter lacks a name: field; all other commands in the repo include name: in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add 'name: review' to the frontmatter

pattern: WebSearch declared but not evidently used in handoff document creation
description: WebSearch is listed in allowed-tools but the command's task (creating a context handoff document from conversation history) does not require web search
suggested_fix: Verify if WebSearch is used; if not, remove it from allowed-tools to reduce permission surface
